title: viola tricolor subsp. vivariensis
intro: Viola tricolor subsp. vivariensis, also known as heart's ease or heartsease, is a subspecies of the pansy plant, characterized by its small, delicate flowers with a mix of yellow, purple, and white colors. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Viola tricolor subsp. vivariensis include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as an antiseptic, making it a valuable natural remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
